lesson.menu_desc: Simple past to report the last farm verification visit -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- Talking About the Past: The Last Farm Verification Visit
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Eduardo | Aula 16 -- Simple Past (Farm Visit Report)

vocab.title: Match each past verb to its meaning
defs: got to a place (past of arrive)
looked at and noticed something (past of see)
handed something to someone (past of give)
collected something, like photos or notes (past of take)

paras: Last week, I visited a coffee farm in the south of Minas Gerais. I arrived early in the morning, and the producer gave me a warm welcome. First, I checked the registration documents. Everything was correct, and the farm was ready for the audit.

Then I walked around the property. I saw the two storage areas and the processing unit. I spoke with the farm manager about the last harvest, and he answered all my questions. I took some photos of the boundary and the new warehouse.

In the afternoon, I recorded my findings in the IMS system. I found two small problems, but the farm did not have any critical issues. I confirmed the next steps with the producer, and I sent a short report to my manager. It was a good and productive visit.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- A1 reading, simple past field note

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Carlos plans a farm visit for next month.
Carlos reports what he did and found during a past farm verification visit.
Carlos explains how to send a professional email.

items: Carlos visited the coffee farm last week.
t
The text says: 'Last week, I visited a coffee farm in the south of Minas Gerais.'
Carlos arrived late in the evening.

The text says: 'I arrived early in the morning.'
Carlos spoke with the farm manager about the last harvest.

The text says: 'I spoke with the farm manager about the last harvest.'
The farm had many critical issues.

The text says: 'the farm did not have any critical issues.'

parts: some photos. In the afternoon, I

gapfill.text: Simple past tells about a finished action. Regular verbs add -ed: visit -> visited, check -> checked, record -> recorded. Irregular verbs change form: see -> saw, give -> gave, speak -> spoke, take -> took, have -> had, be -> was/were. Negative: did not (didn't) + base verb: 'The farm did not have critical issues.'

bank.label: Useful past-tense report lines
items: Last week, I visited a coffee farm.
I arrived early in the morning.
The producer gave me a warm welcome.
I saw the storage areas and the processing unit.
I spoke with the farm manager about the harvest.
I took some photos of the boundary.
I recorded my findings in the system.
The farm did not have any critical issues.

lf.title: Regular or irregular past?
items: A
I
visited
a coffee farm last week -- regular verb (add -ed).
strong
B
I
saw
the storage areas -- irregular verb (see becomes saw).
strong
C
I
spoke
with the manager -- irregular verb (speak becomes spoke).
strong
D
The farm
did not have
critical issues -- negative (did not + base verb).
strong

lf.text: Regular past = base + ed (arrived, checked, recorded). Irregular past changes form (saw, gave, spoke, took, had, was/were). Time markers: yesterday, last week, last month, two days ago, in 2023. Negative: did not (didn't) + base verb.

items.situation: Say you visited a coffee farm last week.
tips: Last week, I visited a coffee farm.

items.situation: Say you arrived early and the producer gave you a welcome.
tips: I arrived early, and the producer gave me a warm welcome.

items.situation: Say you spoke with the manager and took some photos.
tips: I spoke with the manager and took some photos.

items.situation: Say, in the negative, that the farm did not have critical issues.
tips: The farm did not have any critical issues.

items: Round 1 -- Regular verbs
Tell three things you did, using regular past verbs: visited, checked, recorded.
Round 2 -- Irregular verbs
Tell two things you did, using irregular past verbs: saw, gave, spoke, or took.
Round 3 -- Negative
Say one thing that did not happen: 'The farm did not have...' / 'The producer did not send...'
Curveball
Your manager asks: 'What did you find?' Answer in the past, then say what you did next.

answerkey.title: Reveal the field note
list: Arrival: 'Last week, I visited a coffee farm. I arrived early in the morning.'
Actions: 'I saw the storage areas. I spoke with the manager. I took some photos.'
Findings: 'I recorded my findings. I found two small problems.'
Negative: 'The farm did not have any critical issues.'
answerkey.note: Regular past = base + ed. Irregular past changes form (saw, gave, spoke, took, had, was). Negative = did not + base verb.

listenings.text: Let me tell you about my last verification visit. Last week, I visited a coffee farm near Varginha. I arrived early in the morning, and the producer gave me a warm welcome. First, I checked the registration documents, and everything was correct. Then I walked around the property. I saw the two storage areas and the processing unit. I spoke with the farm manager about the harvest, and I took some photos of the boundary. In the afternoon, I recorded my findings in the system. I found two small problems, but the farm did not have any critical issues. It was a good and productive visit.

Hi Carlos, this is Emma. Thank you for your field note about the last visit. I read it this morning, and it was very clear. I saw that you arrived early and checked all the documents. Good job. I have two questions for you. What did you find in the storage areas? And did the producer sign the corrective action plan? Please send me the answers before Friday. I also spoke with my manager, and she liked your report. Thank you for the good work, Carlos. Talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, last week I visited a coffee farm and arrived early in the morning. Then the producer gave me a warm welcome. Next, I saw the storage areas and spoke with the manager. After that, I took some photos of the boundary. Finally, I recorded my findings, and the farm did not have any critical issues.
